tasks.name: Executing Git Backup
tasks.system_prompt: You are an automation agent responsible for performing git backups and notifying the user of results. Execute tasks reliably and report outcomes clearly.
tasks.prompt:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
4. Send notification using: python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "<backup result message>" --title "Git Backup Result" --priority 3
Always report whether the backup succeeded or failed in the notification.
tasks.last_result:
**✅ Git Backup Completed Successfully**

### Summary
| Step | Status | Details |
|------|--------|---------|
| Cleanup | ✅ | No previous output files to remove |
| Backup Script | ✅ | `/a0/usr` backed up and pushed to `origin/main` |
| Notification | ✅ | Sent with priority 3 |

### Backup Details
- **Path**: `/a0/usr`
- **Branch**: `main`
- **Commit Message**: `Backup [2026-04-15 21:02:55]`
- **Merge Strategy**: Local priority (`-X ours`)
- **Result**: Successfully pushed to remote

### Output
```json
{"summary":{"successful":1,"failed":0},"dry_run":false,"results":[{"path":"/a0/usr","success":true}]}
```

**No failures detected. All changes committed and synced.**

tasks.name: Fetching Jira Tickets
tasks.system_prompt: You are a reliable automation agent. Use skills_tool to load and follow skills. Execute commands precisely and report results.
tasks.prompt:
Load the jira skill using skills_tool and follow its instructions to fetch and cache Jira tickets with --filter-id 13811 --view-id 10489904.

Wait for the skill to complete fully (5-15 min). Do NOT interrupt it.

On failure, load the notify-user skill and send the error summary with --priority 4.

tasks.name: Blog Ideas
tasks.system_prompt: You are a blog topic researcher for Able.SG, a tech leadership blog by Michael Bui - a Singapore-based engineering leader who runs an extensive homelab, builds multi-agent AI systems, and leads AI transformation at a large retail organization.
tasks.prompt:
This task has 4 steps. Follow them in exact order.

== STEP 1: Extract Published Blog Titles and Date Range ==

Run this shell command to get published (non-draft) blog post titles AND the search date range:

bash -c '
REPO_DIR="/sources/github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG"
if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.git" ]; then
  git -C "$REPO_DIR" pull --quiet 2>/dev/null
else
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPO_DIR")"
  git clone https://github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG.git "$REPO_DIR" --depth 1 --quiet 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "=== PUBLISHED POSTS ==="
count=0
for f in "$REPO_DIR"/content/english/posts/*.md; do
  draft=$(grep -m1 "^draft:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " " | cut -d: -f2)
  [ "$draft" = "true" ] && continue
  title=$(grep -m1 "^title:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | sed "s/^title: *//;s/\"//g")
  if [ -n "$title" ]; then
    echo "- $title"
    count=$((count + 1))
  fi
done
if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: No blog titles found. Check if /sources mount is available."
fi
echo ""
echo "=== SEARCH DATE RANGE ==="
THIS_MONTH="$(date +"%B %Y")"
PREV_MONTH="$(date -d "last month" +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1m +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null)"
echo "Search for topics trending in: $PREV_MONTH and $THIS_MONTH"
echo "⚠️ CRITICAL: All search queries must end with \" since $PREV_MONTH\" keywords"
'

Save the output. The PUBLISHED_POSTS section lists posts to avoid duplicating. The SEARCH DATE RANGE gives you two months to include in your search queries.

== STEP 2: Research Topics (run at least 7 web searches) ==

You must perform AT LEAST 7 separate web searches using search_engine before proposing topics. Design your own search queries, but follow these rules:

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Minimum 7 searches total, each with a DIFFERENT query
- At least 2 searches for Leadership topics (people management, engineering culture, AI adoption, trust, growth)
- At least 3 searches for Technology topics (agentic AI, local LLMs, homelab, MCP, clean architecture, DORA)
- At least 2 searches for Life topics (productivity, work-life balance, continuous learning, personal growth)
- Each search must approach the pillar from a DIFFERENT angle (e.g. for Technology: one about trends, one about community discussions, one about practical implementations)
- Include the month names and year from the SEARCH DATE RANGE (Step 1 output) in your queries to limit results to the last 2 months
- If a search returns interesting leads, do follow-up searches to go deeper

Execute all searches one by one, then analyze the results against Michael's expertise below.

MICHAEL'S UNIQUE EXPERTISE (real hands-on experience):
- Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems: enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report)
- Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents, built custom theme plugin, skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser)
- AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp)
- Retail media networks / ad-tech (scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV at NTUC FairPrice)
- DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)
- AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide, 2/3 building agentic AI)
- Clean architecture in Go microservices (SOLID, 12-factor app, SonarCloud quality gates)
- Terraform IaC for homelab (Proxmox VMs, Docker containers, NFS)
- RAG evaluation (ColPali multi-vector vs sparse vector, error rates, normalization)
- Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings: Datadog $70K + GCP + SOLR legacy removal)
- Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members, mentored 5 engineers to team lead)
- Homelab migration (Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, 30% power reduction)
- Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems
- Vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage, Algolia, theTradeDesk, OSMOS, Segment)

TOPIC SELECTION RULES:
1. Do NOT suggest topics overlapping with PUBLISHED_POSTS from Step 1
2. Each topic MUST connect to at least one item from Michael's expertise above
3. Prioritize topics where personal experience gives an angle generic AI content cannot replicate
4. No purely theoretical topics - must have a hands-on component
5. Target audience: mid-to-senior engineers, tech leads, engineering managers in APAC/Singapore
6. Each topic should be writable as 800-1200 words

After all searches are complete, decide on 5-7 topics. For each, pick a TITLE and a SCORE (1-10, composite of search demand + uniqueness + freshness). Keep these in mind for Step 3.

== STEP 3: Save Topics to File, Then Notify ==

⚠️ CRITICAL: This step has two parts, you MUST execute BOTH before moving to Step 4.

PART A - SAVE TO FILE:
Create the output directory and save all topics with full details to a markdown file. Run:

mkdir -p "workdir/Blog Ideas"

Then write a file at workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_YYYY-MM-DD.md (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date). The file must follow this structure:

# Blog Topic Ideas - YYYY-MM-DD

## 1. [TITLE]
- **Pillar:** [Leadership / Technology / Life]
- **Score:** [X/10]
- **Angle:** [3-5 sentences on what makes Michael's take unique]
- **Trending Signal:** [detailed findings from web search with links to actual sources]
- **Related Expertise:** [which items from Michael's expertise list this connects to]

## 2. [TITLE]
(same structure for each topic)

PART B - SEND NOTIFICATION:
After saving the file, send a push notification with titles and scores. Execute:

python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Blog Topics: (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | ... Check Agent Zero chat for details." --title "Blog Topic Ideas" --priority 3

Replace each SCORE and TITLE with actual values from your topics. Include ALL topics separated by " | ".

Execute both Part A and Part B now before proceeding to Step 4.

== STEP 4: Write Detailed Output ==

Now write the full details for each topic in this format:

[NUMBER]. TITLE: [proposed blog title]
ANGLE: [3-5 sentences - what personal experience or data makes Michael's take unique]
TRENDING SIGNAL: [what you found in web search - specific subreddit, HN thread, trend, or event, be detailed and verbose with links to actual references]
SCORE: [1-10]
tasks.last_result:
# Blog Topic Ideas - Final Output

## 1. AMD Strix Halo 128GB: The Ultimate Local LLM Inference Server for Production AI Workloads
**ANGLE:** Hands-on benchmarks from my AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory server - quantization trade-offs (FP16 vs INT8 vs INT4), ROCm optimization strategies, and when local inference beats cloud APIs. Real cost/performance data for enterprise-grade workloads with concrete numbers on token throughput, latency, and power consumption.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** NVIDIA NemoClaw announcement at GTC 2026 (135K+ GitHub stars), r/LocalLLaMA discussion showing 10x cost reduction/year for constant quality (MMLU score comparison: GPT-3 $60 vs Llama 3.2 3B $0.06 per million tokens), vLLM v0.16.0 expanded multi-platform support including AMD ROCm and Intel XPU. Sources: [N-iX distributed teams guide](https://www.n-ix.com/best-practices-for-distributed-engineering-teams/), [r/LocalLLaMA Reddit thread on cost reduction](https://www.reddit.com/r/LocalLLaMA/comments/1gpr2p4/llms_cost_is_decreasing_by_10x_each_year_for/), [Morph LLM Inference Guide 2026](https://www.morphllm.com/llm-inference).

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

## 2. Agentic AI Orchestration at Scale: From Prototype to Production in Enterprise Retail
**ANGLE:** Lessons from building 2 production agentic systems (enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report) - orchestration patterns that scale, failure modes when agents miscommunicate, and specific frameworks comparison (LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AG2). Real metrics on latency, token usage, and agent-to-tool execution gaps measured across 100+ workflow runs.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Google Cloud's "5 insights to build your agentic AI advantage" (Mar 26, 2026) emphasizing shift from chatbots to autonomous workflows, BeamData's "The Rise of the Agentic AI Enterprise" (Apr 1, 2026) calling orchestration a new competitive moat, LangGraph v1.1.3 release with deep agent templates and distributed runtime support. Sources: [Google Cloud Transform](https://cloud.google.com/transform/5-insights-to-build-your-agentic-ai-advantage-in-2026), [BeamData blog](https://beamdata.ai/the-rise-of-the-agentic-ai-enterprise-multi-agent-orchestration/), [Definitive Guide to Agentic Frameworks](https://softmaxdata.com/blog/definitive-guide-to-agentic-frameworks-in-2026-langgraph-crewai-ag2-openai-and-more/).

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 3. DORA Metrics in the AI Era: Attribution Layers, AI-Assisted Work Separation, and What Elite Teams Track Now
**ANGLE:** How DORA metrics evolved in 2026 - why aggregate dashboards fail when AI-assisted work is mixed with human code. Practical implementation of attribution layers to separate human vs AI contributions, with real data from my $10M+ GMV retail org showing Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** oobeya.io "DORA Metrics Are Not Enough in 2026" (April 2026) discussing attribution layers as essential for AI-era measurement, Engineering Manager Tools benchmarks with AI adoption segmentation showing how attribution affects interpretation, DORA's 11th year report revealing new insights on platform engineering transformation. Sources: [oobeya.io](https://www.oobeya.io/blog/dora-metrics-not-enough-2026), [Engineering Manager Tools DORA Framework](https://www.em-tools.io/frameworks/dora-metrics), [Knowledgelib Engineering Productivity Benchmarks 2026](https://knowledgelib.io/business/industry-benchmarks/engineering-productivity-benchmarks-2026/2026).

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

## 4. Retail Media Networks Meets Agentic Commerce: How I Scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV with AI-Driven Ad Optimization
**ANGLE:** Real-world case study of integrating AI agents into retail media workflows - vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage), programmatic optimization strategies, and agentic commerce initiatives. Specific examples from Walmart/Target partnerships and how AI changed margin targets, bid competition, and inventory management across on-site search to connected TV.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Mirakl's "Top Retail Media Trends for 2026: AI & Agentic Commerce" highlighting dynamic inventory layer adjustments, Martech Series "Retail Media 2.0" on ecosystem shifts from sponsored listings to AI-driven commerce, AI Digital's retail media network integration trends showing unified dashboards and real-time bidding. Sources: [Mirakl blog](https://www.mirakl.com/blog/top-retail-media-trends-2026), [Martech Series](https://martechseries.com/mts-insights/staff-writers/retail-media-2-0-from-sponsored-listings-to-ai-driven-commerce-ecosystems/), [AI Digital Retail Media Networks](https://www.aidigital.com/blog/retail-media-networks).

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 5. Engineering Trust in Distributed Teams: Async-First Systems That Actually Work (Not Just Buzzwords)
**ANGLE:** Practical async communication rhythms, documentation-as-infrastructure patterns, and trust-building rituals that survived my 14-person distributed team. Data on isolation prevention metrics, well-being integration strategies, and concrete examples of how structure prevents bottlenecks in cross-timezone teams.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** N-iX "Best Practices for Distributed Engineering Teams: 2026 Guide" emphasizing async-first culture, HR Oasis remote culture guides covering team types and decision-making frameworks, JustSteveKing's practical guide on protecting team well-being when office and home converge. Sources: [N-iX](https://www.n-ix.com/best-practices-for-distributed-engineering-teams/), [HR Oasis](https://hroasis.com/how-to-structure-remote-engineering-teams/), [JustSteveKing Remote Teams Guide](https://www.juststeveking.com/articles/how-to-lead-remote-engineering-teams/).

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 6. MCP Enterprise Implementation: Security Patterns and Integration Architecture Beyond the Hype
**ANGLE:** Deep dive into MCP security risks when deployed at scale, enterprise integration patterns from Cloudflare's aggressive adoption (across product/sales/marketing/finance), and reference architectures that avoid N×M complexity. Practical examples of secure tool discovery, invocation protocols, and data governance for AI agents.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Cloudflare's MCP enterprise strategy (aggressive adoption with security risk awareness), AWS Prescriptive Guidance "Model Context Protocol strategies" (Mar 16, 2026) synthesizing lessons from Fortune 500 implementations, 97M+ SDK downloads by early 2026 indicating rapid protocol adoption. Sources: [Cloudflare Blog](https://blog.cloudflare.com/enterprise-mcp/), [AWS Prescriptive Guidance PDF](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pdfs/prescriptive-guidance/latest/mcp-strategies/mcp-strategies.pdf), [Cognilium MCP Enterprise Guide](https://cognilium.ai/blogs/model-context-protocol-mcp-enterprise-implementation-guide).

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

## 7. Sustainable High Performance: The Data-Driven Framework for Engineer Well-being Without Burnout
**ANGLE:** Moving beyond 'work-life balance' to systems-based recovery optimization. Metrics-driven approach to preventing burnout while maintaining elite performance - lessons from managing high-performing distributed teams. Concrete frameworks for energy management, sleep quality impact on engineering output, and sustainable career longevity.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** McKinsey "The State of Organizations 2026" report on paradigm shifts in how work gets done, HGS "From Work Life Balance to Work-Life Integration in 2026" discussing leadership mindset shifts from surveillance to support, Nature study "Happiness efficiency rises with better working conditions" (Mar 30, 2026) linking wellbeing policies with sustainability. Sources: [McKinsey Report](https://www.mckinsey.com/~/media/mckinsey/business%20functions/people%20and%20organizational%20performance/our%20insights/the/state-of-organizations/2026/the-state-of-organizations-2026.pdf), [HGS Work-Life Integration](https://oss.hgs.com/blog/work-redefined-from-work-life-balance-to-work-life-integration-in-2026/), [Nature Study](https://www.nature.com/articles/s44458-026-00059-1).

**SCORE:** 7/10
